title: Pyrus chengkangensis
intro: Pyrus chengkangensis, also known as Chengkang pear, is a species of plant in the family Rosaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus chengkangensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pyrus chengkangensis include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Pyrus chengkangensis is a species of pear tree in the family Rosaceae. It is a flowering plant that belongs to the genus Pyrus, which consists of several species of pear trees. The family Rosaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that includes many fruits, such as apples, pears, and plums. This classification is based on the plant's morphology, anatomy, and molecular characteristics. Pyrus chengkangensis is native to Yunnan province in China.
